item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I personally feel this can be further… Elaborated? By telling us whether the researcher did something to his wife, or the man involved.

But nonetheless, this is good. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes, there is an answer. We plan to write an article about it in future.

I'll include a mini-spoiler, since you asked:

+ Spoiler
- Title for hiding text

Somewhere else, an individual found three "oracles" in his attic (I'll keep the nature of the "oracles" a mystery for now, unless you really want to know). They had been passed down from generation to generation (he is related to SCP-3574-C).

When he found them, he unintentionally activated their anomalous property. All three will foretell a terrible future, then each will state methods to prevent it, but only one method is correct. Performing the other two methods (or not doing anything) will result in the tragedy happening.

The Foundation contained these three "oracles", and classified them as "Safe", but later "Euclid", because they activated spontaneously one day, and recited the riddle in the message, indicating that there is a fourth "oracle". Furthermore, they state that something terrible will happen, unless the fourth one is found and contained.

While the first three "oracles" simply foretell tragedies, and ways to prevent them, the fourth is malevolent, and causes these tragedies, to feed off misfortune.

Neither the individual, nor the group related to SCP-3574 knew about the existence of each other's "oracles". They were created by their ancestor, to save them from persecution, or some other reason, which we haven't decided.

The nature of the "oracles" is hinted in the pictures of the decapitated goat heads, that accompany the riddle.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So is there an answer to the riddle and would you reveal it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you very much, glad you liked the read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting glimpse at what motivates the 'oracle's' handlers/owners/servants. The mild surprise and veiled annoyance at the researcher's refusal to follow the script was well conveyed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you very much, I was also thinking of MC&D during writing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like this should belong to some…subsidiary of MC&D. In a good way.

Either way, this was pretty good. There's room for improvement, narratively, and some of your tone needs cleanup, but this was good in a quirky way, enough for me to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fixed; thank you for pointing that out.

Thank you for the encouragement!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think there's a little room for improvement, but overall, it's a better execution of a similar idea I've had for a while. +1 for encouragement, also there's a line in the interview said by Kershoff that should be -C.
